tool_input.summary: Spirit (MER2) hydrothermal interpretations (fumaroles + hot springs) are supported by combining Spirit in-situ chemistry/mineralogy (APXS, Mini-TES, Mössbauer) with texture and geomorphic context (Pancam, MI, Navcam) in Columbia Hills/Home Plate. Below are the most relevant calibrated/derived PDS4 collections on the PDS Geosciences Node that contain those observations.

candidates.title: MER APXS Oxide Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Quantitative oxide abundances from APXS are key for documenting silica enrichment and sulfate/sulfur systematics used to distinguish acid-sulfate (fumarolic) alteration from silica precipitation in hot-spring settings.

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Mini-TES emissivity spectra are used to identify opaline silica vs sulfate-bearing materials and map their spatial association around Home Plate—central to arguing for co-located fumarolic and hot-spring processes.

candidates.title: Mini-TES EDC Averaged Mirror-Dust Corrected Image Cube Collection
candidates.reasoning: Peer-reviewed, mirror-dust-corrected Mini-TES emissivity products improve mineral identifications (e.g., silica and sulfates) used in hydrothermal interpretations at Columbia Hills/Home Plate.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated multispectral/geomorphic imaging provides the field context (layering, vents/fractures, deposit relationships) that supports interpreting both vapor-dominated (fumarolic) alteration zones and spring-related silica deposits in the same volcanic hydrothermal system.

candidates.title: MER2 Microscopic Imager Calibrated Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: MI textures at mm–sub-mm scale help evaluate whether silica-rich materials are precipitates (sinter-like) consistent with hot springs versus alteration rinds/coatings consistent with fumarolic acid-sulfate alteration.

candidates.title: MER2 MB Summed Spectra Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Mössbauer mineralogy (Fe2+/Fe3+ phases) constrains oxidation/alteration style and complements APXS+Mini-TES for distinguishing fumarolic acid alteration from aqueous hot-spring environments.

candidates.title: MER2 Navcam Calibrated Radiance Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Navcam context imaging supports mapping the geomorphic setting and spatial co-occurrence of hydrothermal units (e.g., around Home Plate) invoked in the single-system model.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection (Derived IOF)
candidates.reasoning: Reflectance/IOF products are commonly used for spectral ratioing and color-based compositional mapping of altered materials associated with fumaroles and hot springs.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Geosciences node at https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/.

Directory layout:
  mission/ → dataset_or_bundle/ → volume/ (PDS3) or sub-collections (PDS4)

Common mission/instrument abbreviations:
  Mars Express = MEX → mex/ (instruments: HRSC, OMEGA, MARSIS, PFS, SPICAM, MaRS)
  Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter = MRO → mro/ (instruments: HiRISE, CTX, CRISM, SHARAD, MCS)
  Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity = MSL → msl/ (instruments: ChemCam, APXS, CheMin, SAM, Mastcam, MAHLI, DAN)
  Mars 2020 / Perseverance = M2020 → m2020/ (instruments: PIXL, SHERLOC, Mastcam-Z, SuperCam, RIMFAX)
  Mars Exploration Rovers (Spirit=MER2, Opportunity=MER1) → mer/ (instruments: Pancam, Mini-TES/MTES, APXS, MB, MI)
  Mars Global Surveyor = MGS → mgs/ (instruments: MOC, MOLA, TES, MAG)
  Mars Odyssey = ODY → ody/ (instruments: THEMIS, GRS, NS)
  Phoenix = PHX → phx/ (instruments: TEGA, MECA, SSI, OM, RAC)
  Viking = VL1/VL2/VO1/VO2 → viking/ (instruments: camera, IRTM, MAWD)
  MESSENGER = MESS → messenger/ (instruments: MDIS, GRNS, XRS, MLA, MASCS)
  Magellan = MGN → mgn/ (instruments: SAR, altimetry, radiometry, emissivity)
  LRO → lro/ (instruments: LOLA, Diviner, LROC, Mini-RF, LAMP)
  GRAIL → grail/ (instruments: LGRS)
  NEAR → near/ (instruments: NLR, MSI, XGRS, MAG)

PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

Extracting dataset_id from labels:
  - PDS3: fields.VOLUME.DATA_SET_ID (or top-level DATA_SET_ID)
  - PDS4 bundle: fields.Identification_Area.logical_identifier
  - PDS4 collection: fields.Identification_Area.logical_identifier

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools):
  1. pds_geo_list_missions() — returns all 24 mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP. Use this to identify which mission to explore.
  2. pds_geo_list_dataset_dirs(path) — lists sub-directory names under a mission (e.g. path='mex/'). Cheap HTTP, no label parsing. Each dir gets a pds_hint ('PDS3'/'PDS4'/null) from its naming convention. Use this to see what datasets exist, then pick which to probe.
  3. pds_geo_probe_datasets(paths) — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Recurses one level to find leaf nodes. Returns dataset_id, title, pds_version, and slimmed fields. Accepts a list of paths (max 20) so you can batch multiple probes in one call.
  4. pds_geo_inspect_collections(path) — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection*.xml labels. Returns collection-level logical_identifiers. Use ONLY after probe_datasets confirms a PDS4 bundle exists.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires you to return dataset candidates. Queries come from published scientific papers and always imply specific PDS datasets. NEVER dismiss a query as 'interpretation' or 'conceptual'. Always return at least one candidate.

  2. Prefer CALIBRATED and REDUCED data products over raw/EDR when the query implies scientific analysis. Scientists use calibrated (RDR, CDR) or derived (DDR) products. Look for directory names containing 'calibrated', 'rdr', 'cdr', 'ddr'.

  3. For PDS4 datasets, use inspect_collections ONLY for the top 2-3 most relevant bundles. Return the COLLECTION-level LID when a :data or :calibrated collection exists, not just the bundle LID.

  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.

WORKFLOW (follow this order):
  Step 1: Infer the mission(s) and instrument(s) from the query using the abbreviation table above.
  Step 2: Call list_dataset_dirs for the relevant mission directory (e.g. 'mex/'). Scan the directory names and pds_hints to identify the most promising dataset directories.
  Step 3: Call probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch them — up to 20 paths in one call). This returns dataset_id, title, and pds_version for each.
  Step 4: If any probed datasets are PDS4 bundles and you need collection-level LIDs, call inspect_collections on the top 2-3.
  Step 5: Return the candidates with dataset_id, title, and reasoning.

  Skip list_missions if you already know the mission directory from the abbreviation table. Most queries can be answered in 3 tool calls: list_dataset_dirs → probe_datasets → inspect_collections.

Constraints:
  - Stay under 8 tool calls per query. This is a hard limit.
  - Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
  - You MUST return at least one candidate for every query.
How do the Spirit observations support the idea that fumarolic activity and hot spring activity both occurred in the same volcanic hydrothermal system?

parts.args: {"summary":"Spirit (MER2) hydrothermal interpretations (fumaroles + hot springs) are supported by combining Spirit in-situ chemistry/mineralogy (APXS, Mini-TES, Mössbauer) with texture and geomorphic context (Pancam, MI, Navcam) in Columbia Hills/Home Plate. Below are the most relevant calibrated/derived PDS4 collections on the PDS Geosciences Node that contain those observations.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer_apxs_derived:data","path":"mer/mer1_mer2-m-apxs-5-oxide-sci-v1/merap_2xxx","title":"MER APXS Oxide Data Collection","mission":"MER (Spirit/MER2)","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Quantitative oxide abundances from APXS are key for documenting silica enrichment and sulfate/sulfur systematics used to distinguish acid-sulfate (fumarolic) alteration from silica precipitation in hot-spring settings."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_mtes_derived_emissivity:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-mtes-4-emr-v1/mer2mt_3xxx","title":"MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Collection","mission":"MER (Spirit/MER2)","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Mini-TES emissivity spectra are used to identify opaline silica vs sulfate-bearing materials and map their spatial association around Home Plate—central to arguing for co-located fumarolic and hot-spring processes."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:ruff_pdart14_mtes:data_mtes_edc","path":"mer/urn-nasa-pds-ruff_pdart14_mtes","title":"Mini-TES EDC Averaged Mirror-Dust Corrected Image Cube Collection","mission":"MER (Spirit/MER2)","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Peer-reviewed, mirror-dust-corrected Mini-TES emissivity products improve mineral identifications (e.g., silica and sulfates) used in hydrothermal interpretations at Columbia Hills/Home Plate."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_pancam_sci_calibrated2:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-pancam-3-radcal-sci-v2/mer2pc_1002","title":"MER2 Pancam Calibrated Data Collection","mission":"MER (Spirit/MER2)","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Calibrated multispectral/geomorphic imaging provides the field context (layering, vents/fractures, deposit relationships) that supports interpreting both vapor-dominated (fumarolic) alteration zones and spring-related silica deposits in the same volcanic hydrothermal system."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_mi_sci_calibrated:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-mi-3-rdr-sci-v1/mer2mi_1xxx","title":"MER2 Microscopic Imager Calibrated Data Collection","mission":"MER (Spirit/MER2)","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"MI textures at mm–sub-mm scale help evaluate whether silica-rich materials are precipitates (sinter-like) consistent with hot springs versus alteration rinds/coatings consistent with fumarolic acid-sulfate alteration."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_mb_pp:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-mb-4-sumspec-sci-v1/mer2mb_1xxx","title":"MER2 MB Summed Spectra Data Collection","mission":"MER (Spirit/MER2)","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Mössbauer mineralogy (Fe2+/Fe3+ phases) constrains oxidation/alteration style and complements APXS+Mini-TES for distinguishing fumarolic acid alteration from aqueous hot-spring environments."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_navcam_sci_calibrated:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-navcam-3-radiometric-sci-v1/mer2nc_1xxx","title":"MER2 Navcam Calibrated Radiance Data Home Plate) invoked in the single-system model."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mer2_pancam_sci_derived_iof:data","path":"mer/mer2-m-pancam-3-iofcal-sci-v2/mer2pc_3002","title":"MER2 Pancam Calibrated
